steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts a sex-specific difference: immature females should direct a higher proportion of peering events at permanent residents than males do, because females stay in their home region while males disperse. Supporting data must show females outpacing males on that proportion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Individual 6 and individual 1 directed a higher proportion of peering events at permanent residents of their home regions than did individual 13 and individual 15.": individuals 6 and 1 are female (higher proportions: 0.67 and 0.59); individuals 13 and 15 are male (lower proportions: 0.15 and 0.00). This directly shows the female-higher, male-lower sex pattern the hypothesis predicts. ✓
  • "The proportion of peering events directed at permanent residents of the immature orangutans' home regions ranged from a low of 0.00 to a high of 0.67.": reports an overall range without any sex breakdown — does not support a sex-specific hypothesis. ✗
  • "Individual 6 directed a higher proportion of peering events at permanent residents of its home region than did individual 1, and individual 13 directed a higher proportion of peering events at permanent residents of its home region than did individual 15.": compares within-sex pairs, not between sexes — irrelevant to the hypothesis. ✗
  • "Individual 13 engaged in 27 peering events, more than either individual 6 or individual 1.": counts total events, not proportions directed at permanent residents — the wrong metric for the hypothesis. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
